route.name: Pitkin Passage
route.description: Public GPX mountain route with six passes, rough doubletrack, singletrack, and hike-a-bike potential.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX mountain route with six passes, rough doubletrack, singletrack, and hike-a-bike potential.
route.bestSeason: Late June to early October; July works only after snowmelt and with storm checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX mountain route with six passes, rough doubletrack, singletrack, and hike-a-bike potential.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Creeks and ponds are frequent in the high country; filter everything.
resources.food: Basic provisions in Pitkin and summer services in Tin Cup; verify hours.
resources.camping: Abundant dispersed camping, but exact legal camps need to be chosen from the GPX and land-manager context.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until snow and thunderstorm risk are checked.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact legal campsite proof; private mining claims and Tincup camping restriction are material.
No current pass/trail snow proof for multiple 12,000 ft passes.
Active Stage 2 fire restrictions; no campfire/charcoal plan required.
No July 9-10 final weather, smoke, lightning, and closure refresh.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Map exact campsites and verify public land/camping legality outside restricted/private parcels.
Check GMUG alerts and Tincup Camping Restrictions immediately before departure.
Check COTREX/local reports for Cumberland, Tin Cup, Tunnel Lakes, Tomichi, Canyon Creek, Black Sage, and Waunita.
Refresh NWS point forecasts for Pitkin, Tin Cup Pass, Tomichi Pass, and Black Sage within 24-48 hours.
Refresh BLM/USFS fire restrictions, InciWeb, and smoke forecasts on July 9-10.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: In-season but very hard; snow, storms, and hike-a-bike exposure require explicit acceptance.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
